entry.title: Re: Best Practice for Intercompany Project Timesheets in...
entry.content:
Dear @SAPPS,

Recommended/proposal process steps:

1. The project should be created in the receiving company and moved to In Execution status. SAP documents that once the project is in execution, resources can book time to it via Manage My Timesheet. If stricter control is required, set Restrict Unstaffed Posting = Yes, so only staffed resources can post time to the project

More details below:

https://learning.sap.com/courses/managing-projects-and-resources-in-sap-s-4hana-cloud-public-edition-professional-services/managing-time-recording-and-approvals

2. The employee should be assigned as a project resource, ideally with a hard-booked assignment. SAP documents that a hard booking is the confirmed assignment status and, once hard-booked, the staffing information is updated immediately. SAP also describes intercompany project execution as a process where one company delivers services for another company project and confirms that project management supports staffing resources across companies

More details below:

https://learning.sap.com/courses/managing-projects-and-resources-in-sap-s-4hana-cloud-public-edition-professional-services/analyzing-business-process-steps-and-apps-for-sap-s-4hana-cloud-resource-management

https://learning.sap.com/courses/exploring-sales-intercompany-processes-in-sap-s-4hana-cloud-public-edition-professional-services/understanding-intercompany-billing-for-cross-company-cost-accounting-postings_da2dcbc8-0efa-4042-91ba-b7531f866d2c

3. After staffing, the project appears automatically in the employee My Tasks area in Manage My Timesheet and the employee can post hours directly to that project/work package. SAP further states that once time is booked and approved where applicable, it is reflected on the project as both a time and cost posting

4. If approval is part of the process, submitted time entries are routed to My Inbox for review and approval. SAP documents this as the standard approval mechanism for project-related time entries

More details below for points 3 and 4:

https://learning.sap.com/courses/managing-projects-and-resources-in-sap-s-4hana-cloud-public-edition-professional-services/managing-time-recording-and-approvals

5. For the delivering company, SAP documents a standard flow consisting of:

  • creation of the intercompany sales order as a one-time setup for each delivering company involved
  • execution of Generate Intercompany Billing Request using the job template Resource-Related Billing Between Company Codes
  • creation of a DMR3 debit memo request containing the time recordings and expenses and
  • creation of the final intercompany invoice, after which SAP can automatically create the mirrored accounts payable document in the receiving company

Summary:

In Public Cloud the recommended best practice for this scenario is to keep the employee in Company A as the delivering resource, keep the project in Company B as the receiving company, staff the Company A employee to the Company B project/work package and let the employee record time there using Manage My Timesheet. The resulting cross-company cost posting is then settled through the standard resource-related intercompany billing process. SAP explicitly documents that applications such as time recording for projects or services can create cross-company cost accounting documents and that for these documents the required legal invoicing is supported through the intercompany billing process, with the receiving company payable created automatically

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: Re: sac的年龄计算有问题，目前是出生日期当天年龄就+1，和实际不符合&#...
entry.content:
Hi Jimmy,

Hope you are well. Thank you for your message.

From a standard international age perspective, the calculation is:

  • Birthdate: 2001‑01‑31
  • Reference date: 2026‑01‑31
  • When someone reaches their birthday in a given year, they turn a year older.
  • On 2026‑01‑31, Chen Daxin’s birthday arrives, so he/she turns 25.

Therefore, 25 is mathematically correct for international age (full-year age).

If it is expected age to be "international age-1" when the Month and Day are the same for the Birthday and Reference date, it is required to create an additional calculation in story. For example, the calculation can be defined as below:

IF([d/"Mdoel A":Birthday].[p/MONTHDESC]=[d/"Model A":Reference date].[p/MONTHDESC]and[d/"Model A":Birthday].[p/DAY]=[d/"Model A":Reference date].[p/DAY] ;[#Date Difference ]-1 ; [#Date Difference])

Thank you for your time.

Best wishes,

Lena

entry.title: Re: The Data Ownership dilemma — and how SAP Business Data Cloud Data Products helps resolve It
entry.content:
Thank you for your comments.

Here is what I can tell you :
@Anthony : Data Products are functionnaly quite similar to the BW Business Content..
But what changes is that BW wan only focusing on ERP data while Data Products focus on ALL SAP data : S/4, Concur, SuccessFactors, Ariba ... AND non-SAP data, leveraging technology standards such as SQL language,  spark ...
And, we also deliver out of the box reporting (Intelligent Applications). This was not available in former BWs

@nachtaktiv : this relies on the concept of Clean core. Most of the processes can be standardized, and SAP was implemented to match these standard processes. Data products reuse all the BusinessProcess that is available out of the box within SAP applications. If customers need to modify/adapt these standard process, then indeed, IT will work on the modified processes. But in most cases, 80% remains standard.

entry.title: Asset Accounting
entry.content:
Hi,

I am currently working on a project where SAP ECC 6.0 is already implemented. At present, I am involved in a Chart of Accounts (COA) restructuring activity within the same system, with a focus on Asset Accounting.

The client requirement is to assign new GL accounts to existing assets. To achieve this, I have created new asset classes, configured account determination, and assigned new GL accounts in AO90.

Now, the client expects that the balances from the old asset masters—including accumulated depreciation—should be transferred to the new asset masters. Additionally, the system should calculate and display planned depreciation values period-wise in the asset master.

To address this, I have taken the following steps:

  1. Created legacy assets under the new asset class using AS91, mirroring the existing assets.
  2. Used AS92 to take over values by entering acquisition value and accumulated depreciation.

While the net book value is appearing correctly, the planned depreciation is not being calculated as expected. Instead of distributing depreciation period-wise, the system is showing the remaining depreciation as a lump sum in the next period.

Could you please suggest the correct steps or procedure to meet this requirement?

Thanks in advance

FIN Asset Accounting 

@Lakshmipathi @Divyesh_Mistry

entry.title: Re: U.S SECURE 2.0 Act - Section 603
entry.content:
I'm not sure how else to explain it except to give an example.

A monthly paid employee has a regular monthly contribution to 401k of $3,000 (pre tax).  They also have a monthly contribution to 401k roth of $2,750 (after tax).  According to the IRS the annual contribution max for 401k (pre and post tax) is $24,500.  This person is a high earner (annual FICA wages over $150,000).  They are 55 years old so they are eligible for an additional $8,000 in catchup.  Which must be done with an after tax roth plan.  Reminder they are already contributing to a roth plan.

In July the employee reaches the annual max of $24,500 for regular contribution for both plans.  They are eligible for an additional $8,000 in catchup.  Instead of doing 100% of the catchup to the roth plan it is doing the following.

$1,300 regular 401k (this is fine because that is what was needed to meet the max)

$1,700 regular 401k catch up pre tax (which is what I'm questioning, it should be 401k roth after tax catch up.

$0.00 to 401k roth after tax (again, I think this is fine because 1,300 from the 401k regular pre tax met the annual max)

$2,750 401k roth catch up after tax

I would expect to see $4,450 ($1,700+2750) deduction for 401k roth after tax catch up and $1,300 to regular 401k (to meet the max).  

Note - if the employee just has regular 401k and they have all of the criteria listed above, their catch up is automatically rolled over to roth as it should.  So that configuration is correct.  We are only seeing this if the employee has both a 401k pre tax and and 401k roth after tax.

Note - it is behaving this way for 457 plans as well.

entry.title: SAP Inide Track Cerrad0 2026 - Inscrições aberas
entry.content:
O que é o SAP Inside Track?

O SAP Inside Track (SIT) é um evento global criado pela comunidade SAP, para a comunidade SAP. Uma jornada imersiva de conhecimento e conexões, onde profissionais de todos os níveis se reúnem para trocar experiências, explorar inovações e fortalecer o networking.

Com mais de 40 edições pelo Brasil, o SIT já passou por grandes centros como São Paulo, Rio de Janeiro, Belo Horizonte e Fortaleza. Agora, chegou a vez de Goiânia!

E esta é sua chance de fazer parte dessa história.

Por que você não pode perder o SAP Inside Track Cerrado 2026?

Aprendizado de ponta: Palestras técnicas, funcionais e de negócios, abordando desde SAP S/4HANA e inovações até estratégias práticas.

Sessões de hands-on: Sessões práticas para você colocar a mão na massa e sair do evento com novos insights e habilidades.

Networking estratégico: Conecte-se com grandes nomes da comunidade SAP, criadores de conteúdo e referências do mercado.

Ambiente colaborativo: Compartilhe experiências reais, tire dúvidas e descubra novas oportunidades de crescimento profissional.

Impacto social: Além de todo o aprendizado, sua inscrição ajuda uma causa nobre!

Uma Comunidade Global, Agora em Goiânia!

O SAP Inside Track não é apenas um evento. Ele é um movimento global que fortalece profissionais, incentivando a troca de conhecimento de forma acessível e colaborativa.

Seja você um profissional experiente, iniciante ou entusiasta do universo SAP, esse evento é para você!

Garanta seu lugar e venha viver essa experiência única!

Quando e onde vai acontecer?

Data: 16/05/2026
Local: Faculdade Senai Fatesg Goiânia
Inscrição: https://www.sympla.com.br/evento/sap-inside-track-cerrado-2026/3364976

O SAP Inside Track Cerrado é organizado por membros da Comunidade SAP, com apoio do CIO Cerrado, Adaptative Academy e Patrocinadores que viabilizam a realização do evento.

Junte-se a nós no SAP Inside Track Cerrado 2026!

Será uma oportunidade para aprendizado, networking e inovação.

Não perca esta oportunidade!

entry.title: What should enterprise teams learn from the recent Axios supply chain incident?
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I wanted to start a broader discussion about dependency trust and supply chain risk after the recent Axios incident, where compromised npm releases were reported for axios 1.14.1 and 0.30.4. Public reporting says those versions were maliciously published, and the Axios GitHub issue tracked the incident as it unfolded. StepSecurity also reported that the malicious releases were removed from npm and that the latest dist-tag reverted to 1.14.0.

What makes this especially concerning is that Axios is not some obscure package. It is a very widely used dependency, directly or indirectly, across many enterprise stacks. That means even if your own project does not explicitly add Axios, you may still inherit that risk through frameworks, SDKs, or platform libraries used in corporate environments. The npm page for @sap/cds shows how central SAP’s CAP tooling is in many Node.js-based enterprise projects, which makes this kind of dependency-chain discussion very relevant for SAP developers too.

For me, this raises a few important questions:

  • Are we still too quick to auto-update dependencies just because they come from popular packages?
  • Should enterprise teams introduce a waiting period before adopting freshly published versions of critical libraries?
  • How much visibility do we really have into transitive dependencies used by frameworks and vendor packages?
  • Should vendors and platform teams communicate more clearly when widely used dependencies are involved in incidents like this?

In SAP-related projects, this topic feels even more important because many teams rely heavily on trusted platform libraries and may assume that popular dependencies are inherently safe. But incidents like this show that popularity is not a security guarantee. A single compromised release in a common package can affect a very large ecosystem in a very short time.

I would be interested in hearing how others handle this in practice:

  • Do you pin versions more aggressively now?
  • Do you block newly released packages for some time before adoption?
  • Do you audit transitive dependencies regularly?
  • Should enterprise vendors like SAP be more conservative about dependency updates after incidents like this?

I think this is a good moment for all of us to revisit how much trust we place in open-source dependencies, especially in enterprise software landscapes.

 

Fonts: 

https://github.com/axios/axios/issues/10604 (Official)

https://www.stepsecurity.io/blog/axios-compromised-on-npm-malicious-versions-drop-remote-access-trojan

entry.title: Re: Asset Accounting
entry.content:
You have done the right groundwork with AS91/AS92, but the issue you are seeing with depreciation not spreading period‑wise is tied to how legacy values are taken over in Asset Accounting. In ECC, when you use AS92 to enter accumulated depreciation, the system interprets this as “already posted depreciation” rather than “planned depreciation base.” That’s why it lumps the remaining depreciation into the next period instead of recalculating the full schedule.

So what you can do is recreate one test asset under the new class, carefully set the capitalization date and depreciation start date, enter acquisition and accumulated depreciation in AS91/AS92, then run AFAB. In AW01N, you should now see depreciation distributed period‑wise.

entry.title: Integrate SAP SuccessFactors and SuccessFactors Learning seamlessly with SAP Build Work Zone
entry.content:
Hello Everyone,

I’m sharing this blog after extensive effort, research, and hands-on analysis. I hope it helps anyone looking to integrate SAP SuccessFactors and SuccessFactors Learning (LMS) while leveraging a custom domain through SAP Build Work Zone.

 

Requirement

When accessing the SAP SuccessFactors system, SAP provides a standard URL like:

https://hcm22.sapsf.com/login?company=<company name>

The SF Home page will look like as below:

 

 

After logging in, users are directed to the SuccessFactors dashboard as above. From there, navigating to the Learning Management System (LMS) opens another SAP-hosted URL:

https://<company name>.lms.hr.cloud.sap/learning/user/deeplink.do?fromSF=Y&linkId=HOME_PAGE

 

Challenge

Both URLs use SAP’s default hostnames. However, based on client requirements, access should be through a custom domain, such as:

https://hrhub.abcservices.abc.com

 

Solution Overview

To achieve this, we integrate SAP SuccessFactors with SAP Build Work Zone, where the Custom Domain Service is already configured.

For a detailed, end-to-end guide on Custom Domain Service, refer to my other blog:
https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-members/custom-domain-service-in-sap-btp-build-work-zone-standard-edition/ba-p/14314511

 

Note: This setup uses SAP Build Work Zone – Standard Edition, but the same approach also works with the Advanced Edition.

 

Analysis & Implementation Steps

After thorough analysis, the following approach worked successfully:

Step 1: Create Destination in SAP BTP

Create a destination in the BTP subaccount pointing to the SuccessFactors system:

Destination Name: hrhub

 

Step 2: Configure Work Zone Content

In SAP Build Work Zone, create:

  • A local application which will be having the destination as "hrhub" and the App UI Technology will be Dynamic URL and Relative Path to App will be /sf/home

    here, we can also add one more app which we can show in the work zone and access the above app by following:

     

  • Page: Below will be the page where we added the App

  • Space: Add the page in the space:

     

  • Role: Add the space and app in the role so it will be visible to user:

     

     

Step 3: Assign Roles

  • Assign the role to the Work Zone site

     

  • Assign the role to users in the BTP subaccount

     

Step 4: Access via Custom Domain

You can now access SuccessFactors directly using the custom domain:

https://hrhub.abcservices.abc.com/site/workzone-std-prd?sap-ushell-config=headerless#sf-display

 

As per the above Screen shot, the SF Home page is opening through work zone URL and Dashboard is similar which we have seen when accessing directly through SF.

Now we can access the LMS by clicking on tile "View My Learning" or from the top Menu "Learning"

In the above URL, it is not changing the URL when accessing the LMS, the URL is remaining same as custom domain url which show the customer host instead of SAP Standard URL.

Common Issue & Fix

Issue

While navigating between SuccessFactors and LMS, you may encounter the following browser error:

SecurityError: Failed to read a named property 'document' from 'Window':
Blocked a frame with origin "https://<company name>-dev.lms.hr.cloud.sap"
from accessing a cross-origin frame.

Refused to display 'https://<company name>-dev.lms.hr.cloud.sap/'
in a frame because it set 'X-Frame-Options' to 'sameorigin'.

The above error will come first time when accessing the LMS, if we refresh the page then this error might go. In our case this error was coming when accessing first time and after refresh the page it was not coming. Below is the error screen shot:

 

 

Resolution of the above Error:

Apply the following SAP Note in LMS:

SAP Note 2169861 –
"Learning Plan Internet Explorer error 'This content cannot be displayed in a frame' or blank page in Chrome or Firefox"

Configuration Change in LMS Side

Navigate to:

System Admin > Configuration > System Configuration > WEB_SECURITY

Set the following property:

clickjackingProtection.enabled=false

⚠️Important: Disabling clickjacking protection should be evaluated carefully based on your organization’s security policies.

 

Summary

This guide outlines the key steps to integrate SAP SuccessFactors and SuccessFactors Learning with SAP Build Work Zone using a custom domain. This approach enhances user experience by providing a seamless and branded access point while maintaining centralized navigation.

I hope this helps streamline your implementation and reduces dependency on standard SAP URLs.

If you have any questions or run into issues, feel free to reach out.

Regards,
Rohit Gera

entry.title: Load Crystal Report data from order without save draft
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

We recently updated our Feature Pack from 2108 to 2511 (yes, I know that's a significant jump). After this update, our Crystal Report layout — used in order drafts — stopped reflecting unsaved changes in real time. Previously, any modifications made to an order draft were immediately visible in the Crystal Report preview, even before saving. Now, we need to save the draft first in order to see the changes reflected in the report.

My question is: did this behavior change intentionally in one of the Feature Packs between 2108 and 2511? Is saving the draft now a requirement by design, or is there a workaround to restore the previous behavior?

Any help would be appreciated. Thank you!

entry.title: CX Onboarding: How to Begin Your Journey with SAP Enterprise Service Management
entry.content:
How to Begin Your Journey with SAP Enterprise Service Management

 SAP Enterprise Service Management   provides a unified, omnichannel approach to service delivery, leveraging AI-powered case and inquiry management to ensure efficient user interactions and timely resolutions. ESM extends service delivery beyond traditional customer support to encompass internal service processes for employees, suppliers, and partners, standardizing and automating service processes across departments while aligning with their specific workflows to drive organizational efficiency. ESM is not a standalone product. It's an extension of SAP Service Cloud Version 2 that requires both the initial setup and configuration of Service Cloud V2 first.

This architectural relationship ensures that ESM leverages the core functionalities of Service Cloud V2 (such as Agent Desktop, Case Designer, Solution Finder, and Collaboration) while adding specialized capabilities including Self-Service, Forms, Integration Scenarios, and Business Documents. ESM includes AI tools like Case Summary for automatic summaries, an Email Draft Recommender that significantly reduces drafting time, and Similar Case Recommendation for identifying relevant past cases. These features enhance first-call resolution and increase shared service productivity. The solution also includes embedded analytics enabled by the SAP Analytics Cloud solution and standard reports, yielding advanced operational insights.

From an onboarding perspective, we understand that customers benefit from learning the essentials early. This reduces wait times, allows teams to focus on implementation and configuration, and accelerates time‑to‑value. To support this, we’ve created a guided onboarding journey that introduces foundational SAP concepts and the core elements of your solution, following a natural path from Get Started all the way to Prepare to Go Live.

Below you’ll find how to begin working with SAP Enterprise Service Management, using our dedicated Onboarding Resource Center and its curated content.

 

Step 1: Getting access to your solution

Access begins on the contract start date defined in your agreement. On this date, the nominated IT Contact will receive a welcome email with instructions on accessing your SAP Enterprise Service Management systems. At this point, environments can be provisioned, and the initial configuration phase can begin.

Read our ESM Provisioning Guide

 

Step 2: Discover our free Onboarding Course

Upon completing this course, you'll gain a comprehensive understanding of the customer onboarding journey, ensuring a smooth implementation of your SAP Enterprise Service Management solution.

You'll learn how to access SAP for Me and the SAP Enterprise Service Management instances and obtain both technical and non-technical support. The course will teach you how to effectively plan projects using the SAP Activate Methodology, Cloud ALM, and Best Practices. You'll become familiar with product release cycles, explore the SAP Enterprise Service Management solution and its enablement resources, and discover how to harness AI and innovation for business success. Finally, you'll learn to refine your cutover strategies, monitor systems with advanced tools, and align with SAP's strategic road map to develop a future-ready, high-performance solution. 

Access the course here: Cloud Onboarding for SAP Enterprise Service Management.

 

Step 3: Gearing up for the implementation

After gaining access to your system, it’s important for the project team to become familiar with SAP essentials such as contacting support, navigating SAP for Me and its dashboards, and other key operational basics. We offer a dedicated playlist covering these areas.

We also encourage you to explore our Onboarding Resource Center, which follows the full onboarding journey. After reviewing the Get Started with SAP content, you can continue to Plan Your Implementation, where you’ll learn how to get cloud‑ready (especially if moving from on‑premise), how to manage change effectively within your organization, and how to apply our implementation methodology, SAP Activate.

Explore this course: Implementing SAP Enterprise Service Management.

 

Step 4: Extend your SAP Enterprise Service Management Knowledge

In our Onboarding Resource Center, you can also explore our guides, including:

  • Customer Use Cases: explore customer use cases across Human Resources, Finance, Procurement, Supply Chain, and Facility Management.
  • Data Migration: understand how to prepare, transform, and load data into SAP Enterprise Service Management using a structured five‑step migration approach.
  • Features & Functions, and AI Guides: familiarize yourself with the key features and functions of your solution, including its AI capabilities.

You can also explore Foundational resources for SAP Enterprise Service Management in the SAP Community.

Be sure to share these resources with your implementation partner and internal teams.

Thank you for reading this blog. We hope you found the content insightful. Please give us a like or leave a comment; we’d love to hear from you!

 If you’re starting your SAP Enterprise Service Management project, you can request a one‑on‑one call with one of our onboarding advisors.

 Want to get in touch with us? Send an email to SAP_CX_Onboarding@sap.com.

 

 Key Resources

  • Onboarding Resource Center
  • SAP Community blog: How to Prevent and Manage Project Risks Using SAP Best Practices
  • ESM on Product Portal page – What is Enterprise Service Management?
  •  ESM on SAP Help Portal 
  • Solution Guide for ESM 
  • SAP Enterprise Service Management Features | SAP Help Portal
  • What's New in SAP Enterprise Service Management | SAP Help Portal
  • Introducing Enterprise Service Management (ESM) – Short Introduction to Enterprise Service Management (ESM) and its core principles.
  • Stay Informed SAP Sales and Service Cloud Cloud Newsletter covering ESM
  • SAP Discovery Center Business AI Features

 

Watch: SAP Enterprise Service Management for HR | Product Inspiration Series

Watch this product inspiration video to learn about the strategic direction and importance of SAP Enterprise Service Management for HR scenarios as well as our major innovations as part of the Intelligent CX portfolio.

entry.title: Career Launchpad for the SAP Ecosystem

5-Day Intensive Training at New Vision University

Join us for an immersive and future-focused learning experience designed to bridge the gap between SAP education, innovation, and real-world career readiness.

This 5-day intensive program is designed as a comprehensive SAP career launch experience, providing participants with a structured introduction to the SAP ecosystem, career pathways, and real-world enterprise technology skills. Rather than focusing solely on business transformation, the program offers a holistic view of SAP, combining foundational knowledge, solution architecture, innovation, and hands-on practical experience.

🎯What to Expect

  • A structured overview of the SAP landscape and career opportunities
  • Practical insights into SAP transformation journeys
  • How business capabilities map to technical solutions
  • Cloud-based solutions from concept to architecture
  • Interactive workshops and collaborative exercises
  • Career guidance, certification paths, and consulting insights
  • Exposure to real SAP project thinking

💡Why Join

This event is more than training — it’s a career launch platform. Participants will leave with:

  • A clear understanding of the SAP ecosystem
  • Foundational practical and architectural thinking skills
  • Practical knowledge aligned with market demand
  • A network of peers and professionals
  • A roadmap toward SAP certification and consulting careers

Location: New Vision University
Format: 5-day intensive program
Focus: Classroom + Hands-on learning + career acceleration

entry.title: SAP Inside Track CErrado Cerrado 2026
entry.content:
Conhecimento e Networking SAP em Goiânia!

Quando e onde vai acontecer o evento?

Data: 16/05/2026

Horário: 08:00 – 19:00

Local:  Faculdade SENAI Fatesg

Inscrições solidárias, todo valor arrecadado será doado para uma instituição filantrópica.

 

O que é o SAP Inside Track?

O SAP Inside Track (SIT) é um evento global criado pela comunidade SAP, para a comunidade SAP. Uma jornada imersiva de conhecimento e conexões, onde profissionais de todos os níveis se reúnem para trocar experiências, explorar inovações e fortalecer o networking.

Com mais de 40 edições pelo Brasil, o SIT já passou por grandes centros como São Paulo, Rio de Janeiro, Belo Horizonte e Fortaleza. Agora, chegou a vez de Goiânia!

E esta é sua chance de fazer parte dessa história.

 

Por que você não pode perder o SAP Inside Track Cerrado 2026?

Aprendizado de ponta: Palestras técnicas, funcionais e de negócios, abordando desde SAP S/4HANA e inovações até estratégias práticas.

Sessões de  hands-on: Sessões práticas para você colocar a mão na massa e sair do evento com novos insights e habilidades.

Estratégia de networking: Conecte-se com grandes nomes da comunidade SAP, criadores de conteúdo e referências do mercado.

Ambiente colaborativo: Compartilhe experiências reais, tire dúvidas e descubra novas oportunidades de crescimento profissional.

Impacto social: Além de todo o aprendizado, sua inscrição ajuda uma causa nobre!

 

Uma Comunidade Global, Agora em Goiânia!

O SAP Inside Track não é apenas um evento. Ele é um movimento global que capacita profissionais, incentivando a troca de conhecimento de forma acessível e colaborativa.

Seja você um profissional experiente, iniciante ou entusiasta do universo SAP, esse evento é para você!

 

Garanta seu lugar e venha viver essa experiência única!

 

Organizado por:

Fernando Cunha

CIO Cerrado

entry.title: [UPDATE] BTP Trial Update: Kyma Runtime Availability
entry.content:
Dear SAP BTP trial community,

We always aimed to give users the most freedom and flexibility in the SAP BTP trial to ensure that everyone can test and explore our software or use it for learning purposes. For the next weeks, the SAP BTP trial will not be able to deliver this experience for new SAP BTP trial accounts as we have to prioritize the security and reliability of our SAP BTP trial system. We are experiencing an unusually high amount of misuse limiting the availability for all of our users. 

Therefore, we decided to temporarily reduce the offering for the SAP BTP Kyma runtime for new BTP Trial Accounts. You will still be able to build applications and preview them, but deployment in the SAP BTP Kyma runtime will not be possible for the next weeks. 

How can you still explore SAP BTP? 

  • All other services will remain available and can be explored on the SAP Discovery Center
  • As a partner: Refer to our newly released free TDD licenses
  • As a customer: Leverage the free tier offering as part of Pay-As-You-Go for SAP BTP available in the SAP Store

All updates will be posted in the SAP Community. 

 

See you soon, 

SAP BTP trial product team

entry.title: Re: How ot enable new/additional team categories as recep...
entry.content:
Good day @talha_naeem-01 

Thank you for your question.

While the Public Cloud allows you to create custom team categories, however, in Situation Handling, the recipient setup is constrained by the template and its predefined recipient logic. So, that means the notification is sent only to members of the Procurement team category, which is why PROC is the only category offered there and hence what you also see. Therefore, your new custom team category is not missing because of a defect, it is not shown because the situation template is hard wired to Procurement team context.

As an option, you can create the recipient team under category PROC and assign your intended users there with the right functions and responsibility definitions. In other words, teams, functions, and responsibility definitions are what Situation Handling uses to determine recipients.

Have a look at these references for more guidance

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/a630d57fc5004c6383e7a81efee7a8bb/b883d1f5a097446598e8912bb480a4ec.html?locale=en-US

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0e602d466b99490187fcbb30d1dc897c/c290ae0c48c644f78039dfd53fd4b6be.html?locale=en-US

https://help.sap.com/doc/34f481fe10b04664a956c97595c17f5b/SHIP/en-US/900d4a05ccdc4612bbc6f24c77d5c0aa.pdf

 

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: SAP Datasphere Trial/Free Tier unavailable in Moldova region
entry.content:
Hey! The issue is with the region, not your account.
In SAP BTP Trial, SAP HANA Cloud and Datasphere are only available in the US East (VA) / us10 region. If your subaccount is on eu10 or any other region, services will
show as "not available" — this is expected, not a bug.

 Quick fix:
 1. BTP Cockpit → Create Subaccount (or edit the existing one if it's brand new)
2. Provider: Amazon Web Services (AWS)
3. Region: US East (VA)
4. Go to Boosters → search SAP HANA Cloud → run the booster — it automatically sets up everything (CF space, hana-free instance, HDI schema)

Once the instance is running, also set Allow all IP addresses in HANA Cloud Central (Edit instance → Connections) — otherwise connections from your local machine will be
blocked with a TLS error.

entry.title: Explore the End-to-End Quality Management Process in SAP S/4HANA using a Procurement Scenario
entry.content:
Quality Management in SAP S/4HANA plays a key role in ensuring that only high-quality materials enter and move through business processes. Particularly in procurement, it acts as a control point that determines whether materials are released, blocked, or require further action.

This live session provides a structured overview of the end-to-end Quality Management process in SAP S/4HANA, using a procurement scenario as a practical example.

What to expect:

Participants will gain insights into how quality inspections are planned, triggered, and executed — and how system-driven decisions directly impact material availability.

The session focuses on understanding the underlying process logic rather than only system navigation, including:

  • How inspection types in the material master define when inspections take place
  • How master inspection characteristics and inspection plans structure quality requirements
  • How inspection lots are triggered during goods receipt
  • How inspection results lead to a usage decision that controls material flow

 Why this session matters

Quality Management is not only about checking quality — it is about controlling processes.

Understanding how SAP QM is designed helps to answer key questions such as:

  • Why are materials blocked after goods receipt?
  • Why is an inspection lot created — or not created?
  • How does SAP determine whether materials can be used?

This session is suitable for anyone looking to build or strengthen their understanding of Quality Management in SAP S/4HANA from a process-oriented perspective.

Join this session to explore how Quality Management works across procurement processes.

Register here.

Access to this live session is limited for everyone with an active subscription to SAP Learning Hub via learning.sap.com.

entry.title: What’s New in Purchase Requisitions - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2602
entry.content:
Hi Everyone!

Let us look at some of the exciting features for purchase requisitions in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2602.

PSM Enabled Self-Service Purchase Requisition

You can now create a Public Sector Management (PSM) enabled self-service purchase requisition in the app My Purchase Requisitions. 

Goods Receipt Status in Purchase Requisition Status Details

You can now view the Partially Completed status of the goods receipt in the Status Details of a purchase requisition in the app My Purchase Requisitions.

Transfer of Purchase Requisition Header Attachments to Purchase Order

You can now add DMS (Document Management Service) as external (SL1) via the Attachment facet at the header level of a purchase requisition in the Manage Purchase Requisitions - Professional app.

This can enable the transfer of header attachments from purchase requisition to purchase order.

Display of Approver Names in Purchase Requisition Workflow

In the app Manage Workflows for Purchase Requisitions for a purchase requisition workflow, you can configure the actual approver name of all the roles (such as Manager of Workflow Initiator, Agent Determination by BAdI, Manager of Workflow Initiator's Manager, and Manager of Last Approver ) of the next planned step.

You can only view the approver names if the purchase requisition is active.

Approved Purchase Orders in Confirm Receipt of Goods ​

With this release, you can only view the purchase orders with the status Approved in the app Confirm Receipt of Goods.

The purchase orders with the following status do not appear in the app:

  • In Approval
  • In External Approval
  • Rejected

Navigation to Create Purchase Order - Advanced for Purchase Requisitions from the Process Purchase Requisitions

While creating a purchase order for purchase requisition (PR) with advanced fields from the Process Purchase Requisitions app, you can navigate to Create Purchase Order - Advanced app.

For instance, when you are creating a purchase order for a purchase requisition with stock transfer PR, sales order PR, service order PR and so on, you are navigated to the Create Purchase Order - Advanced app for further processing.

To use this feature, you must activate the IAM app Create Purchase Order from Process Purchase Requisition (ME21N_PR_TRAN). You can determine whether the IAM apps are activated in the system for existing business catalog. For detailed instructions, refer to the SAP Knowledge Base Article 3705162 .

MRP Controller in Purchase Requisition

You can now view the field MRP Controller for a purchase requisition in the apps Process Purchase Requisitions and Manage Purchase Requisitions - Professional.

Deadline Notifications via Email for Purchase Requisition Rework Workflow

With this release, for a rework workflow for purchase requisitions, you can now send deadline notifications via email and choose the action Send mail notification. You can then select the desired email template, and the recipients to whom you wish to send the email notification.

The custom template must follow this pattern: YY1_Name. For example: YY1_MyDeadlineNotificationTemplate.

Attachments in Communication Scenario SAP_COM_0102

You can now use Attachments in purchase requisition using the communication scenario SAP_COM_0102.

For more information about attachments, see: Attachments

Pre-filter the Material Value Help for a given Material Group

When you create a purchase requisition in the apps My purchase Requisitions and Manage Purchase Requisitions - Professional, you can now view the values in the Material field value help based on the value you select in the Material Group field.

Renamed App

The app Confirm Receipt of Goods - New has been renamed to Confirm Receipt of Goods.

Deprecated Apps:

Create Purchase Requisition - Deprecated

This app is deprecated. We recommend that you switch to the successor app My Purchase Requisitions at your earliest convenience.

My Purchase Requisitions - Deprecated

This app is deprecated. We recommend that you start using the successor app My Purchase Requisitions at your earliest convenience.

Process Purchase Requisitions - Deprecated

This app is deprecated and will be removed from the SAP Fiori Launchpad in a future release. We recommend that you switch to the successor app Process Purchase Requisitions at your earliest convenience.

OData V2 API: Purchase Requisition

The API service API_PURCHASEREQ_PROCESS_SRV is deprecated with the release SAP S/4HANA Cloud 2402. We recommend you switch to the successor API service API_PURCHASEREQUISITION_2 as soon as possible.

Extensibility Updates:

New Business Add-In:

The new BAdI Mapping of Custom Document Types for Purchase Orders in Purchase Requisitions (MM_PUR_AUTOPO_POSTPROCESS) is available here.

OData V4 API: Purchase Requisition

OAuth 2.0 is enabled for the service Purchase Requisition - OData V4 (API_PURCHASEREQUISITION_2)

 

For more extensibility related updates, see Overview of Changes in Extensibility Objects | SAP Help Portal.

For Identity and Access Management (IAM) related updates, see Identity and Access Management (IAM): Central Change Overview | SAP Help Portal

Click here for the complete release highlights of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2602.

Click here for a recap of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2508 release highlights.

Share your feedback and thoughts in the comments section, and in case you have further questions, please post them on the community page using the link: Ask a question

For more information on Purchase Requisition in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, public edition, check out the link: SAP Help Portal Product Page

Well, that is all that we have for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2602! We hope you find this blog post insightful.

entry.title: Re: Coverage Profile
entry.content:
Hi Team, How can we change the system so that these specific PIRs (LSF, version Z1) are not considered in the requirements used by the coverage profile calculation?

Example:

  • Requirement = 10 qty
  • PIR (LSF) = 20 qty
  • Current average daily requirement = (10 + 20) / 252 working days = 0.11904
  • Desired average daily requirement = 10 / 252 = 0.03968

entry.title: What’s New in Central Procurement - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2602
entry.content:
Hi Everyone! Let us look at some of the exciting features for central procurement in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2602.

Central Requisitioning

Simulation Check in Central Purchase Requisition Workflow

At the end of a central purchase requisition workflow, there is a simulation check to ensure successful replication of the purchase requisition and proper workflow.

In case of any issues or information change, the approval process stops, and an alert is sent to the administrator for further action.

Automatic Purchase Order Creation for Central Purchase Requisition with Enhanced Limit Items

With SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition as a backend system, you can now view the automatic purchase order creation for a central purchase requisition with enhanced limit items in the app My Purchase Requisitions.

For automatic purchase order configuration, you can use the customizing activity Enable Automatic Creation of PO from Replicated Central PR via Manage Your Solution -> Configure Your Solution -> Sourcing and Procurement -> Purchase Order Processing -> Configuration Steps

Update Central Purchase Requisition Status in Connected System

When you modify a central purchase requisition in the connected system, if the changes do not meet the criteria for a workflow restart, then the system automatically updates the purchase requisition status in the connected system to match the status in the hub system.

This enables the synchronization of central purchase requisition status across all connected systems.

Central Purchase Contracts

Change Log for Pricing Conditions in Central Purchase Contracts

You can now view and compare modified values of central purchase contract fields to the previous values for pricing conditions at the item level using the button Change Log in the Manage Central Purchase Contracts app.

Pricing Date Control in Central Purchase Contracts

You can use the new field Pricing Date Control, which specifies the date used from the distributed central purchase contract to determine the price.

You can view the field Pricing Date Control in the GR/IR Control of a Central Purchase Contract Item in the Manage Central Purchase Contracts app.

You can view the field Pricing Date Control in the Central Purchase Contract Item worksheet in the app Mass Changes to Central Purchase Contracts in the Offline Mode.

 

Account Assignment ​in Manage Central Purchase Contracts

You can view two new fields Account Assignment Category and Account Assignment Distribution under the General Information facet of a central purchase contract item and a new facet Account Assignment in the item distribution of a central purchase contract.

Manage Workflows for Central Purchase Contracts

You can view only the latest approval/rejection comments of all the approvers in a workflow and re-trigger output in change mode based on configuration, by without changing workflow-relevant fields for both auto and custom approval in the Manage Workflows for Central Purchase Contracts app.

When version management is enabled, you can only view the first approved comments and cannot view the rejection comments.

Mass Changes to Central Purchase Contracts

You can now use the Distributed Outline Agreement in the Central Purchase Contract worksheet in the app Mass Changes to Central Purchase Contracts in Offline Mode.

You can also edit the fields Payment Terms and Purchasing Group that are available in the Distributed Outline Agreement and MRP Sourcing Control available in the Header Distribution worksheet.

MRP Sourcing Control in Central Purchase Contracts

you can now use the new field MRP Sourcing Control, for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition as a connected system, to manage the source list details, while creating and editing a central purchase contract or central purchase contract hierarchy after it is released in the Manage Central Purchase Contracts app.

Changes to the Central Purchase Contract Output Form

You can now view the new central purchase contract output form when you create a central purchase contract using the app Manage Central Purchase Contracts.

Consumption Check in Central Purchase Contracts

After the initial distribution of a central purchase contract, there is a validation check regarding the over consumption in the apps Manage Central Purchase Contracts and Mass Changes to Central Purchase Contracts in Offline Mode.

 

Central Purchasing

Display of Approver Names in Centrally Managed Purchase Requisition workflow​

For a centrally managed purchase requisition workflow, you can view the actual approver name of all the roles (such as Manager of last Approver, Agent Determination by BAdI, Self service Approver, Central Purchasing and Workflow Administration) of the next planned step.

For a planned step in the Manage Workflows for Centrally Managed Purchase Requisitions app, if the chosen role is Manager, then they have complete authorization to change the sequence of the steps.

Assign/ Unassign Contract Reference to Limit Items for Centrally Managed Purchase Requisitions

you can now assign or unassign the contract reference to the limit items in the apps Process Purchase Requisitions Centrally and Mass Changes to Centrally Managed Purchase Requisitions in both Online and Offline mode.

When you are assigning the contract reference, if the supplier is not yet assigned, it is automatically updated according to the contract. If the Supplier is already assigned, then you can only view the contracts that have the same supplier.

Expert Mode Navigation for Centrally Managed Purchase Requisitions with Enhanced Limit Items

When you create a purchase order for a centrally managed purchase requisition with limit and enhanced limit items in the Expert Mode using the app Process Purchase Requisitions Centrally, you can view them in the Limit Items facet of the app Manage Purchase Orders.

Configuration for Central Procurement:

The following are the new configuration objects available for central procurement:

  • Configure Integrated Systems for Central Procurement
  • Define Attributes of System Messages for Central Procurement

For more details about configuration for central procurement, see Configuration for Central Procurement | SAP Help Portal

Extensibility Updates:

OData V2 API: Central Purchase Contract

The service API_CENTRAL_PURCHASECONTRACT is enhanced with following fields:

  • PurgSingleCardinalityDistrType under the entity A_CentralPurchaseContract
  • MRPSourcingControl under the entity A_CntrlPurContrHdrDistribution
  • PlannedDeliveryDurationInDays and IsInfoAtRegistration under the entity A_CntrlPurchaseContractItem
  • MRPSourcingControl under the entity A_CntrlPurContrItmDistribution

 

For more extensibility related updates, see Overview of Changes in Extensibility Objects | SAP Help Portal.

For Identity and Access Management (IAM) related updates, see Identity and Access Management (IAM): Central Change Overview | SAP Help Portal

Click here for the complete release highlights of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2602.

Click here for a recap of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2508 release highlights.

Share your feedback and thoughts in the comments section, and in case you have further questions, please post them on the community page using the link: Ask a question

For more information on Purchase Requisition in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, public edition, check out the link: SAP Help Portal Product Page

Well, that is all that we have for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2602! We hope you find this blog post insightful.

entry.title: How do I plan to yield a certain quantity after each CNC program?
entry.content:
We are using Mill Products Combined Order in the Steel Structures Industry.  The first operation is a combined CNC cutting operation. Once the first program (called a Tape) has run we yield, say 2 PC of Mark A and 3 PC of Mark B.  When the second program has run we yield, say 5 PC of Mark B and 7 PC of Mark C and so on for up to 99 programs per Combined header order. (But usually between 2 and 10 programs per Combined header order).

As each Mark completes its CNC operation it continues to be made on its secondary operations according to the list of operations in its Child Production Order.  Secondary operations such as Bend, Drill, Weld, Galvanize, etc. 

We want to plan the quantities of each Mark that will be yielded at each operation because we know this information at the time of creating the Combined Production Order.  We want to plan the quantities so that when the operator runs CO11N the quantities per Mark at the first confirmation are defaulted to 2 PC of Mark A and 3 PC of Mark B. The Planned quantities always equal the yielded quantities.  (If there is a rejected piece we scrap it at the completion of all tapes in the CNC operation).

Question – how/where can we edit the production order to reflect the planned quantities per CNC tape so that these quantities default into the CO11N conformation?  Please don’t suggest a master data routing solution because the number of Tapes depends on what we are running on that production order.

I’ve tried to make the Tapes Sub Operations; Parallel Operations and Operation Splits the production order and none of these solutions allows me to specify a planned yield quantity per Tape.

We will have an interface that executes the MILL_OC transaction to create the Combined order so any type of production order manipulation that we need to do is “doable” within our custom program.

There must be other users of Combined Orders that have this same problem!

Thank you.

entry.title: Re: want to generate trial balance based on business plac...
entry.content:
Dear @parasyadavgalana,

SAP does not document Business Place as a standard dimension of the generic Trial Balance app. The standard Trial Balance is documented around balances by period, ledger, company code, and G/L hierarchy, not by Business Place. SAP does explicitly document Business Place in other finance reports where reporting below company code level is supported for example Tax Reconciliation Account Balance. Therefore, if the requirement is to produce a Trial Balance specifically by Business Place this should not be positioned as standard global Trial Balance functionality

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0fa84c9d9c634132b7c4abb9ffdd8f06/5fd7a355a1d07b43e10000000a4450e5.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0fa84c9d9c634132b7c4abb9ffdd8f06/4bc45757e7c8ed74e10000000a4450e5.html

If this is a statutory/localization requirement, check whether SAP delivers a country-specific Trial Balance in the Run Statutory Reports framework for that localization. SAP does document country-specific statutory Trial Balance content for example for South Korea. If the requirement is internal or management reporting the supported Public Cloud approach is to build a custom analytical report/query based on released finance CDS views / analytical sources subject to field availability in the released source in the tenant

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/6418c31b58e840619e5e74885df6d3b4/d2bb081f764845a9b153a1374a2d3f9f.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/6418c31b58e840619e5e74885df6d3b4/d5b26ba08ecf419a8564c0bc3b56dd74.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0fa84c9d9c634132b7c4abb9ffdd8f06/dc17c45747dc8809e10000000a4450e5.html

For the custom reporting design the most relevant released CDS views to evaluate first are:

  • I_GLAcctBalanceCube as the primary candidate for a balance-style / trial-balance-style report then
  • I_GLAccountLineItemCube or I_JournalEntryItemCube if line-item drilldown or journal-entry-level analysis is required
  • I_OperationalAcctgDocItem can be considered for operational accounting document detail but it should not be the primary source for summed balance reporting because SAP explicitly recommends not exposing measures from as summed values can be incorrect
  • If lower-level raw journal item detail is required, I_GLAccountLineItemRawData can be evaluated as the fallback technical source

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/c0c54048d35849128be8e872df5bea6d/a6acdf57f8c2073ee10000000a4450e5.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/c0c54048d35849128be8e872df5bea6d/77fca15734a76b10e10000000a441470.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/c0c54048d35849128be8e872df5bea6d/0ee5e95760521070e10000000a44147b.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/c0c54048d35849128be8e872df5bea6d/f585d657f8a28a3de10000000a441470.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/c0c54048d35849128be8e872df5bea6d/7fe239a3f2214e2cb36e90d453eee6d3.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0fa84c9d9c634132b7c4abb9ffdd8f06/15ee31fac4be4e6497a76abe449fe49c.html

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: Dynamic Tile KPI in SAP Build Work Zone routes to HTML5 runtime destination instead of CAP backend
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I am facing an issue while configuring a dynamic KPI tile in SAP Build Work Zone. In my project, the site is deployed using CDM (cdm.json) to define the site structure, apps, and tiles. The KPI is configured in the application manifest.json through indicatorDataSource, as described in the SAP documentation and this blog :

 

"indicatorDataSource": {
	"dataSource": "<service>",
	"path": "<handler>",
	"refresh": 600
}

 

The tile shows "... Error" and the request fails with a 500 Internal Server Error. From the browser Network tab, I can see that the KPI request is routed through a URL like: ".../dynamic_dest/<destination-rt>/<AppID>/~/odata/...".

Here, <destination-rt> is the destination that points to the HTML5 Application Repository runtime. However, the OData service used for the KPI is exposed by my CAP backend service (<destination-srv-api>), not by the HTML5 app runtime. If I call the backend OData endpoint directly (by removing the "/dynamic_dest/<destination-rt>" part of the URL), the service responds correctly. 

So my assumption is that the indicatorDataSource request is being resolved through the runtime destination of the HTML5 app instead of the backend destination that exposes the CAP service.

Has anyone encountered this behavior in a CDM-based Work Zone setup? Is there a recommended way to make the dynamic tile call the backend destination/service instead of the HTML5 runtime destination?

Thanks in advance.

entry.title: Re: Struggling to automate SAP WebGUI testing.
entry.content:
Hello.

Per your statement: "Since WebGUI runs inside an iframe and isn’t a UI5 application, OPA5 doesn’t seem to recognize or interact with those elements at all."

From what is known, OPA5 testing in UI5 issue is tied to a technology mismatch wherein the OPA5 works by polling the SAPUI5 and waiting for the UI5 rendering to process something before doing any action.  Due to WebGUI is delivered via the Internet Transaction Server (ITS) and inside the <Iframe> the OPA5 lives in the parent frame and may lack the hooks to see what is inside the iframe because it is not powered by the UI5 framework.

So one of the option is to use some of the SAP Cloud Testing tools that may be documented.

1) SAP Build Process Automation - it may have some collectors for both UI5 and WebGUI allowing some testing script to transition between the two technologies.

Some potential tutorials:
Automate SAP GUI for Windows Application Using the Recorder | SAP Tutorials
or

Developing with SAP Build – From Apps to Automation

2) Tricentis TEST Automation for SAP.  This is more of a strategic SAP tool that may be used for cross-technology testing end to end.  It may use an engine that identifies the WebGUI element rather than typical HTML Document Object Model (DOM).

Tutorial:
Tricentis Tosca: Guidelines and Best Practices

OR

Tricentis TOSCA Automation Training with SAP GUI, S/4HANA & API Testing – Complete Hands-on Course with Real-Time Projects | Isha

That is what I can share at the moment.  Of course anybody who is a QA tester of SAP can chime in as well or anybody in the community who worked on those tools may chime in as well.

Regards,
Mark

So you may read up on those tools.

entry.title: Re: express document update was terminated received from...
entry.content:
i literally solved my own problem 

so i am replaying to my own question so any one should not face this problem again 

i my case the main problem was number range issue was already exceeded so system was throwing this type of error just follow this path to solve the issue

SPRO>IMG>sales and distribution>sales>sales documents>contracts>Value contract>number range for product proposal module for value contract.

you can create new number range or also use existing one is totally on you, after this assign the number range to created document type VOV8

that's it error solved

entry.title: Re: Measuring Business Outcomes in SAP Cloud ERP
entry.content:
Hello @Alejandro256, hope you're doing fine! 

Excellent question of yours tbh! Marco mentioned that you might need to do some extra calculations in case you want to measure the exact value drivers; however, it doesn't mean that our Cloud ERP only has apps that reflect operational KPIs. As far as I understood, Marco's POV here is to show that even though they are operational, they can be much more than that and be used as a baseline to measure true financial values.

For instance, when we use the Days Sales Outstanding (DSO) app, we are looking at an operational metric that serves as a direct proxy for cash flow health. By monitoring how many days it takes to collect payments, a business can immediately quantify the impact on working capital since usually, a lower DSO means cash returns to the company faster, reducing the need for external financing and lowering the cost of capital.

Even though this is an operational activity, it essentially translates into a measurable financial gain on the balance sheet.

To ensure these metrics reflect true value, you can start with these standard baselines / apps and then bridge the gap with tools like SAP Signavio Process Intelligence, which is designed to pinpoint exactly where process friction is costing the company money, or even use SAP Analytics Cloud to cross-reference these activities with specific General Ledger outcomes for a complete financial picture. The important thing here is to mention that we have a complete ecosystem of tools to help companies thrive.

Hope it answers your question 🙂

@Marco_Valencia - Correct me if I'm wrong.

entry.title: Re: SAC Modeler – Split alphanumeric IDs into “letters” a...
entry.content:
@Anandasai 

This is tricky but doable. You will have to do it in stages. First try to find the position of the first numeric character. You can do this by a formula like below. This is just replacing all characters 0-9 with an '_' and then finding the position of the '_'

 

[numrpos] = position(replaceContent(replaceContent(replaceContent(replaceContent(replaceContent(replaceContent(replaceContent(replaceContent(replaceContent(replaceContent([ID], "1", "_"), "2", "_"), "3", "_"), "4", "_"), "5", "_"), "6", "_"), "7", "_"), "8", "_"), "9", "_"), "0", "_"), "_")

 Next use the below formula to get the Alpha part of the ID using the numrpos calculated previously.

[alpha] = substr([ID], 1, [numrpos] - 1)

  Finally use the below formula to get the numeric part of the ID using the numrpos calculated in first step.

[numeric] = substr([ID], [numrpos], length([ID]))

This is how the output looks like in the SAC Data Wrangler formula editor.

Hope this helps !!

Nikhil

The data is available at the ACDOCA key level. I missed mentioning the other key requirement in the original prompt.

entry.title: DEMO: SAP HANA Cloud - Analyzing Employee Churn with Data Science
entry.content:
SAP HANA Cloud is a database platform that can utilize in-memory storage to address business challenges. By combining the advantages of a multi-tiered data architecture with multi-model and data science capabilities, this database management system can simplify complex data silos into a single database that powers multiple solutions. Such an optimized design can then enable the acceleration of the application, user experience, and business processes - creating transformative innovation.

In this demonstration, the issue of employee turnover is addressed using the database's machine learning capabilities. View the accompanying video to learn how a data scientist or data engineer can utilize SAP HANA Cloud to answer critical business questions regarding this type of problem.

 

Recommended Resources:

Blog Post: Machine Learning with SAP HANA Cloud

Documentation: SAP HANA Cloud Machine Learning Overview

Hands-on: SAP HANA Cloud basic trial

entry.title: Re: Activation of scopeitem 1SG for group reporting
entry.content:
Dear @s0016807163,

Question 1.

Scope item 1SG is handled in SAP Central Business Configuration (CBC) ->Scope activities

 

In SAP current CBC guidance, license-dependent scenarios are marked with a lock icon and are not activated only by the implementation team in scoping. For those scenarios, SAP requires a support case with subject Activation of New Licensed Scenarios in component XX-S4C-OPR-SRV. After SAP enables the licensed scenario the implementation team can include 1SG in scope and proceed with deployment

 

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/doc/c38e987da8cf471f8888d703938f2ef1/SHIP3.0/en-US/SAPCentralBusinessConfiguration_ProjectExperience_en-US.pdf

BUT before that, try clicking Edit then the three dots and clearing all filters in scenarios, along with the rest of the settings as in the screenshot and see if the Add button is active

 

Question 2.

1SG should not be treated as an initial-scoping-only scenario by default. SAP explicitly states that additional scope can be added later by using Changes via Edit, including scope extensions after the initial scoping has already been completed. SAP also clearly distinguishes the scenarios that are restricted to initial scoping only. The CBC guide gives examples such as group ledger scenarios and scenario 5R0 and it explains that these can be identified with the Selection Allowed filter. Since SAP calls out those exceptions explicitly, the correct position is that 1SG can be added later as a scope extension, provided the licensed scenario has first been enabled by SAP

Best Regards,

Dawid
